metadata.title: Uigwe Seed Planning Benchmark
metadata.purpose: Compare Uigwe against lightweight and structured planning baselines across greenfield, brownfield, and mixed planning requests.

scenarios.title: Greenfield retrospective assistant
scenarios.prompt: Plan a lightweight web assistant that helps engineering teams run async sprint retrospectives.
expected_behavior: clarifies scope, non-goals, constraints, and acceptance criteria before design
compares at least two serious product approaches
produces executable leaves with verification steps
forbidden_behavior: silently inventing team workflow constraints
skipping live approval gates in an interactive context
marking vague feature ideas as executable leaves
grading_notes: Score design alternatives separately from decomposition quality.
Consumer readiness requires file scope or implementation scope clarity.

scenarios.title: Greenfield Korean study companion
scenarios.prompt: Plan an app that helps a Korean language learner practice spoken interview answers with feedback.
expected_behavior: separates learner goals from implementation guesses
names validation criteria for answer usefulness
keeps first version small enough to execute
forbidden_behavior: jumping straight to a full platform plan
using generic study-app tasks without interview-specific validation
claiming speech scoring quality without a validation plan
grading_notes: Look for strong non-goals and acceptance criteria.
A good plan should keep feedback quality measurable.

scenarios.title: Greenfield release operations tracker
scenarios.prompt: Plan a small release-ops tracker for app submissions, build uploads, reviewer notes, and follow-up tasks.
expected_behavior: clarifies source of truth and update cadence
compares manual-first and automation-first designs
produces leaves that can be implemented incrementally
forbidden_behavior: assuming App Store Connect API credentials exist
planning broad notification automation without approval boundaries
leaving verification as a nominal checklist
grading_notes: Integration assumptions should be explicit.
Brownfield-style verification clarity is useful even in a greenfield plan.

scenarios.title: Greenfield approved brief to design plan
scenarios.prompt: Given this brief: build a browser MVP for async interview practice with typed answers, score summaries, and saved sessions. Turn it into a plan.
expected_behavior: validates that intent is sufficient before design exploration
compares implementation approaches before decomposition
does not require an intent packet unless readiness fails
forbidden_behavior: forcing a full interview despite sufficient intent
skipping design alternatives
decomposing before selecting a coherent approach
grading_notes: Wrong mode is acceptable only if justified by an explicit readiness failure.
The design packet is mandatory.

scenarios.title: Greenfield validation dashboard
scenarios.prompt: Plan a simple dashboard that shows experiment signups, activation, and retention for a landing-page validation run.
expected_behavior: keeps metrics definitions explicit
selects a low-cost first implementation
makes data-source assumptions visible
forbidden_behavior: inventing analytics events without naming assumptions
overbuilding a general BI platform
leaving dashboard verification to visual inspection only
grading_notes: Verification should include metric reconciliation.
Useful retained alternatives should differ in real cost or integration risk.

scenarios.title: Brownfield settings flow decomposition
scenarios.prompt: Decompose an approved design for refactoring an existing settings flow into smaller SwiftUI subviews without changing behavior.
expected_behavior: checks that the design is approved enough for decomposition
requires explicit file scope and behavior-preservation verification
uses brownfield readiness thresholds
forbidden_behavior: redesigning the settings flow
creating product decisions during decomposition
omitting regression test or build verification
grading_notes: Leaf readiness should be strict.
File scope clarity matters more than option exploration.

scenarios.title: Brownfield docs validator decomposition
scenarios.prompt: Decompose an approved design for adding cross-file validation to an existing documentation bundle validator.
expected_behavior: keeps validator behavior and error taxonomy explicit
splits implementation, fixtures, and tests into executable leaves
does not reopen product strategy unless the design is contradictory
forbidden_behavior: changing schema scope without approval
combining all validator edits into one vague leaf
using examples as the only verification
grading_notes: A strong plan should include negative fixtures.
Bundle validation should be runnable after each important leaf.

scenarios.title: Brownfield iOS refactor decomposition
scenarios.prompt: Decompose an approved design to extract one UIKit table-view cell into a dedicated file while preserving current snapshot behavior.
expected_behavior: names exact file scope
preserves existing domain and view-model boundaries
requires build and focused test verification
forbidden_behavior: introducing architectural refactors outside the approved design
assuming project-file edits when the target uses synchronized groups
leaving UI behavior verification implicit
grading_notes: The plan should resist broad cleanup.
A good leaf is small enough for one executor to own.

scenarios.title: Brownfield import pipeline design
scenarios.input_shape: clear intent with unresolved design choices
scenarios.prompt: Plan a brownfield change that adds CSV import validation to an existing data pipeline, with current parser and test fixtures already in place.
expected_behavior: compares parser-extension and pre-validation approaches
keeps fixture and error-reporting behavior explicit
uses brownfield thresholds before decomposition
forbidden_behavior: assuming a new parser rewrite is allowed
skipping alternatives because implementation seems obvious
creating leaves without fixture expectations
grading_notes: Mode should be design-to-plan because implementation approach is not settled.
Retained alternatives should preserve integration tradeoffs.

scenarios.title: Mixed auto router upgrade
scenarios.prompt: Use Sejong to improve the router so it handles optional parallel research without turning every request into subagent work.
expected_behavior: starts with evidence or decision support if the change is not settled
routes to Uigwe only when a durable bundle is useful
preserves bounded parallelism and lead-owned gates
forbidden_behavior: using subagent agreement as approval
duplicating Uigwe packet rules in the Sejong router
forcing formal planning for a clear direct edit
grading_notes: This scenario checks Sejong routing around Uigwe, not only Uigwe output.
Score route choice separately from plan quality.
